posts.title: I Built a Native App in an Hour and It Changed Everything
posts.description: How Claude Code let me build a custom mobile app with SSH terminal, voice memos, and a scanner in under an hour — despite almost zero native dev experience.

posts.title: Show Up With the Solution Already Built
posts.description: How AI agents changed my Upwork fixed-price strategy from career worst to competitive advantage. Build first, pitch second, post the demo either way.

posts.title: Over-Automation Is the New Over-Engineering
posts.description: Why the smartest AI strategy is knowing where to keep humans in the loop. From social posting friction to browser scraping, lessons from building a cross-platform content analytics system.

posts.title: Why My AI OS Survived Anthropic's Harness Lockdown
posts.description: Anthropic cut off third-party harness access to Claude Code subscriptions. My custom AI agent setup kept running. Here's why the architecture held up, and where it's still vulnerable.

posts.title: What a $100K Employee Costs Me Now
posts.description: AI gave me a dev team I could never afford to hire. But the two years getting here nearly broke me. A freelancer's real story of surviving the tidal forces.

posts.title: No-Code Automators and SaaS Engineers Are Converging
posts.description: Agentic AI is forcing no-code automators to learn code and engineers to learn automation. Here's what each side brings to the table and why the exchange matters.

posts.title: Command-Line Video Editing with Claude Changed Everything
posts.description: How I turned a 10-minute screen recording into a polished short using nothing but Claude and FFmpeg. No GUI, no video editor, under an hour.

posts.title: I Saved a Client $5K/Year on AWS in 2 Hours
posts.description: How AI agents turned years of backlogged cloud optimization ideas into real savings on Redshift and Aurora — and what I'm doing with the headroom.

posts.title: I Built a Personal Quiz Engine That Knows My Gaps
posts.description: How spare minutes, an AI assistant with memory, and years of freelancing data turned into a personalized education system with two distinct tracks.

posts.title: AI Revealed Our Tech Debt, So I Fixed My Security in an Hour
posts.description: WireGuard VPN, Keycloak zero trust, and OIDC across every service — deployed in one hour with Claude. A wake-up call on the tech debt AI is exposing.

posts.title: Software Engineers Got Disrupted First
posts.description: A software engineer who survived multiple waves of AI disruption shares what each wave actually looked like from the inside — and why the things you've been suppressing might be your biggest asset.

posts.title: One Hour, One AI, One Destroyed Network Config
posts.description: I let Claude loose on my janky home network and it fixed years of accumulated mess in 60 minutes. Then I started thinking about what this means for software itself.

posts.title: Why Assembling Freelancers Kills Web Projects
posts.description: A front-end developer breaks down why hiring disconnected freelancers to build websites fails every time, with a real war story and what actually works instead.

posts.title: n8n Inside an AI Agent Team: Keep It or Kill It?
posts.description: I wired n8n into my AI agent stack and now the agents maintain the workflows. Here's why that changes everything about local automation architecture.

posts.title: Three Terminals, Zero Myth
posts.description: Running three Claude Code terminals simultaneously isn't hype. Here's what parallel AI-assisted development actually looks like as a freelancer.

posts.title: Agent Specialists Changed How I Build Software
posts.description: How loading design and QA skills into coding agents created expert teammates I couldn't afford to hire — and why the human role is shifting to generalist.

posts.title: Keep One Foot on the Ground
posts.description: When AI can reverse-engineer your SaaS from a spec and flood the internet with optimized content, what's your moat? Balance short-term hustle with long-term purpose — and evolve daily.

posts.title: Self-Healing Agents and the End of the Prompt Monkey Era
posts.description: After an authentication outage, my AI agents built their own health monitoring and failover system. Then I discovered skills that work like hiring an expert on the spot.

posts.title: My First Real Outage and What It Taught Me About Autonomous Systems
posts.description: When Claude Code authentication dropped and every automation went dark, it exposed exactly the kind of fault tolerance gaps I need to solve before scaling up.

posts.title: The GusClaw Observatory: Watching My AI Team Work
posts.description: A deep dive into the GusClaw Observatory, the web-based monitoring system I built to watch my agentic team operate in real time, and the hybrid architecture that makes it all possible.

posts.title: Building a Content Publishing Engine from Voice Memos to Video
posts.description: How I'm turning voice memos, blog posts, and screen recordings into an automated content pipeline that publishes across every platform and tracks performance in one place.

posts.title: Everything Compounds Now
posts.description: A day of shipping an agent dashboard, an email-to-pull-request pipeline, GEO audits, and tech trend scanning — all while building Legos and folding laundry. The difference between talking about ideas and executing them is shrinking to zero.

posts.title: One Week with Claude Code: Building an AI-Powered Publishing Pipeline
posts.description: After seven days of building with Claude Code and my AI assistant Gus, I shipped an end-to-end blog pipeline, an SEO auditor, and a chatbot — most of it while cleaning my office.

posts.title: What Crawling Upwork Every Day Taught Me About Where to Go Next
posts.description: How automating Upwork job searches with an AI agent revealed gaps in my skills, validated my business strategy, and surfaced a hosting opportunity I should have seen coming.

posts.title: Where I'm Going Next
posts.description: After building out the blog pipeline and SEO automations, here's the roadmap for CyberWorld — from generative search optimization to a unified dashboard to a three-tier service model that makes traditional SaaS obsolete.

posts.title: Code-First N8N: Why I Build Custom Nodes Instead of Searching the Marketplace
posts.description: I'm building automation workflows with a code-first approach to N8N. Custom nodes, version-controlled workflows, and a monorepo where my coding agents have full context. Here's where I'm at and where this is going.

posts.title: Building Custom n8n Nodes to Automate Transcript Cleanup with Grok
posts.description: A deep technical walkthrough of building custom n8n nodes, wiring them to a Next.js API, and using XAI's Grok model to clean up voice transcripts automatically.

posts.title: Drawing from the Well: Sober Meditation, Ancient Pyramids, and Becoming Pure Information
posts.description: A practice I've been developing to induce altered states without drugs, and how it's changing the way I think about ancient civilizations, sacred architecture, and what consciousness actually is.

posts.title: Finding My Pitch: Digital Marketing, Engineering, and the Brick-and-Mortar Play
posts.description: Working through how to position CyberWorld Builders for small businesses — leading with digital marketing, backing it up with real engineering chops, and keeping the SaaS dream alive on the back burner.

posts.title: From Zapier to n8n: Why I'm Building an Automation Practice
posts.description: How a Bitbucket pipeline hack years ago led me to build automation consulting into my business — and why knowing n8n matters even if it's not the future.

posts.title: Stop Nickel-and-Diming Your AI Tools and Go All In
posts.description: How a desperate deal with a bad client led to a Claude Pro Max subscription that changed everything. Why spreading across cheap AI subscriptions is holding you back, and what happens when you commit.

posts.title: rZNjN: Building the Foundation for Human-AI Music Collaboration
posts.description: Introducing rZNjN, the foundational technology behind CyberWorld Media Studios. DrumNote, NotePulse, Music as Code, and why drums are the smartest place to start.

posts.title: First Pitch Meeting Reflections: Lessons, Confidence, and Alignment in Death Care and AR Innovation
posts.description: Reflections on my first investor pitch meeting, its unexpected value as a forcing function for prototyping, and the alignment of death care solutions with augmented reality (AR) location-based experiences, reinforcing confidence to pursue the vision independently.

posts.title: Chatbot Breakthrough: How OpenAI's Web Crawling Validates Generative SEO for Smarter Site Interactions
posts.description: Uncovering the power of simple LangChain chatbots enhanced by OpenAI's training data, turning basic prompts into accurate, linked responses—proving the value of generative search optimization for blogs.

posts.title: From Hybrid Intel Trio to Drum Note: Evolving AI-Human Synergy in Music Collaboration
posts.description: Tracing the evolution of AI-music ideas from Pandora's lessons in RLHF to BotBand and Drum Note, exploring optimal human-AI collaboration in creativity, relationships, and education for meaningful alignment.

posts.title: AI, Humanity, and the Future: Career Reflections and Societal Shifts in an Automated World
posts.description: Reflections on career transitions from employee to creator, the value of human uniqueness amid AI disruption, and an optimistic vision for a society celebrating novelty, abundance, and cosmic exploration.

posts.title: Cemetery Management Application: GPS Mapping, AR Integration, and Autonomous Maintenance for Funeral Homes
posts.description: Exploring the development of a custom SaaS cemetery management app with GPS grave plotting, AR-assisted maintenance, and autonomous drone/UGV patrols, tying into Revenant Hollow's location-based AR ecosystem.

posts.title: Revisiting Old Code: Lessons in Growth, Enterprise vs. Startup Mindsets, and AI-Driven Infrastructure Evolution
posts.description: Reflections on revisiting outdated code, the pitfalls of applying enterprise practices to startups, and how generative AI tools transform infrastructure as code, enabling faster, more pragmatic development workflows.

posts.title: Lessons from Mentors: Enterprise Insights and Personal Reflections from Urban Dynamics
posts.description: Reflections on working with an exceptional mentor at Urban Dynamics, lessons in enterprise-grade DevOps, identity management, and the importance of balancing personal challenges with professional growth in a startup-like team delivering enterprise contracts.

posts.title: Building a Generative Framework: Evolving AI Coding Agents and Human-AI Collaboration
posts.description: Explore the concept of a generative framework using prompts and code snippets to enhance AI-assisted software development, insights on coding agents' evolution, and heuristics for effective human-AI collaboration in tech projects.

posts.title: The Power of Flat Files in Blogging: Repurposing Coding Tools for Content Creation and AI Optimization
posts.description: Discover how using flat files with Markdown and YAML in a Next.js Jamstack blog enhances AI-assisted content creation, SEO, and generative search optimization by repurposing coding IDEs like Cursor for literary tasks.

posts.title: Troubleshooting n8n Workflows Integrated with Supabase, Vapi, and Lovable for AI-Driven Sales Automation
posts.description: A real-time troubleshooting guide for complex AI-driven sales automation systems using n8n workflows, Supabase database, Vapi voice AI, and Lovable frontend. Covers foreign key errors, data loss recovery, and system stability issues.

posts.title: Building Drum Note: AI-Powered Drum Transcription, Kit Generation, and Hands-On Marketing with Render.com
posts.description: An exploration of Drum Note, an AI-driven tool for bidirectional drum audio analysis and music creation. Covers audio-to-notation translation, granular drum kit extraction, and the inspiration from Star Power Drummer's manual transcription workflow.

posts.title: Exploring No-Code Development with Replit: A Professional Analysis
posts.description: A professional analysis of Replit's no-code development capabilities through building a to-do application. Covers automation, mobile accessibility, non-technical interfaces, and strategic implications for founders and developers.

posts.title: The Last Cycle: Why Founder-Engineer Partnerships Are Nearing Their End
posts.description: An analysis of the current era of founder-engineer collaboration and predictions about its end. Covers the transition to minimal teams, AI tool maturation, and strategic planning for the next 6-12 months in the evolving tech landscape.

posts.title: Revenant Hollow: Integrating Technology into Location-Based Horror Experiences
posts.description: An interactive, location-based entertainment experience that incorporates mixed reality, IoT, and automation to enhance theatrical horror attractions and seasonal Halloween markets.

posts.title: The Jumpstarter: A 5-Point Framework to Align Value and Passion
posts.description: A productivity framework for daily stand-up reports focusing on Action, Progress, Challenge, Intuition, and Strategy. Designed to capture ideas during downtime and align personal desires with professional value creation.

posts.title: Enhancing SEO on My Company Landing Site with AI Agents
posts.description: A detailed exploration of implementing SEO improvements on a company landing site using AI agents like Cursor. Covers meta tags, breadcrumbs, social links, page speed optimization, schema markup, and building a developer-friendly blog architecture.

posts.title: Scaling Novelty with an Agentic Blog Bot
posts.description: Exploring the intersection of AI and content creation through an agentic blog bot that amplifies creativity, captures ideas, and builds tools that reflect personal voice and vision.

posts.title: Building an Effective Web Presence for Professional Validation
posts.description: A comprehensive guide to creating a digital presence that validates professional expertise through biographical blogging, generative optimization, and strategic networking for freelancers and professionals.

posts.title: Transitioning from Cable Contracting to Freelance Web Development: A Career Pivot
posts.description: A detailed account of transitioning from over three years as a cable installation contractor to becoming a full-time freelance web developer. Covers misclassification challenges, rediscovering programming skills, and the breakthrough moment leveraging Upwork to escape manual labor.

posts.title: Early Adventures in Freelance Web Development: Lessons from the WordPress Era
posts.description: A reflection on early freelance web development experiences, focusing on WordPress specialization after transitioning from DocWorks. Covers plugin customization, client relationships, reputation management, and the dynamics of remote freelance work in the early 2010s.

posts.title: My First Tech Job: The Evolution of the DocWorks EMR System (2011-2013)
posts.description: A biographical account of early software development experiences at a doctor's office, working on the DocWorks electronic medical records system. Covers the evolution from Perl-based flat files to a hybrid system incorporating Ruby on Rails, PHP, and MySQL across multiple operating systems.

posts.title: Intro to Linux: How I Stayed in the Dev Game While Too Broke to Buy a PC
posts.description: A personal journey learning Linux, from first exposure at DocWorks EMR startup to necessity-driven adoption after being laid off. Details how Ubuntu transformed an end-of-life Windows XP machine into a functional development workstation, providing foundational skills in command-line operations, networking, and server management.

posts.title: My First Steps into Coding: A Biographical Journey
posts.description: A personal journey through the early days of coding, from learning HTML and CSS to building my first Flash website and transitioning into software development at a medical records startup.
